# Implementation Plan: PR Checks Workflow

**Branch**: `002-pr-checks-workflow` | **Date**: 2026-04-01 | **Spec**: [spec.md](spec.md)
**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/002-pr-checks-workflow/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan was generated by the `/speckit.plan` command.

## Summary

Create a GitHub Actions workflow (`.github/workflows/pr-checks.yml`) that validates every pull request with two parallel concerns: scoped plugin test execution (detecting changed plugins via three-dot git diff and running their test suites via dynamic matrix strategy) and PR title conventional commit validation (bash regex matching against the Conventional Commits v1.0.0 pattern). The workflow skips draft PRs, handles empty plugin changes gracefully, and provides clear error messages for invalid PR titles.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: YAML (GitHub Actions workflow) + Bash (inline scripts)
**Primary Dependencies**: GitHub Actions (`actions/checkout`), no external dependencies
**Storage**: N/A (stateless CI workflow)
**Testing**: Shell-based test suite (`tests/run-all.sh` per plugin, shared assertions library)
**Target Platform**: GitHub Actions `ubuntu-latest` runner
**Project Type**: CI/CD workflow configuration
**Performance Goals**: PR feedback within 5 minutes (SC-001), docs-only PRs under 1 minute (SC-004)
**Constraints**: Single workflow file, bash regex (no external actions for title validation), `set -euo pipefail` for all inline scripts, user-controlled context expressions via env vars only (FR-013)
**Scale/Scope**: 1-4 plugins, single repository, solo maintainer

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

| Principle | Status | Notes |
|-----------|--------|-------|
| I. Plugin Structure Compliance | PASS | Workflow detects plugins via `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` presence, aligned with Constitution Principle I |
| II. Script Safety | PASS | All inline bash scripts include `set -euo pipefail` as first executable line (FR-010). Workflow sets `defaults.run.shell: bash`. Constitution II also requires `#!/usr/bin/env bash` shebang and `chmod +x` -- these apply to standalone scripts only; inline GitHub Actions `run:` blocks are not standalone files and are exempt from shebang/chmod requirements. User-controlled context expressions are passed via env vars, not direct interpolation (FR-013) |
| III. Semantic Versioning | N/A | This feature does not modify any plugin versions |
| IV. Test Coverage Before Merge | PASS | No new bash scripts requiring Layer 4 tests. Workflow file itself is validated by running it on a real PR |
| V. Conventional Commits | PASS | PR title validation regex exactly matches the constitution's required types: `feat\|fix\|chore\|docs\|refactor\|test`. The `!` breaking change indicator is positioned per Conventional Commits v1.0.0 spec |
| VI. KISS, Simplicity & YAGNI | PASS | Single file deliverable. Bash regex instead of external action. No speculative features. Two-job detect+test pattern is the simplest approach supporting per-plugin status reporting |

**Post-Phase 1 Re-check**: All gates still PASS. No design decisions introduced complexity beyond what the requirements demand.

### Source Code (repository root)

```text
.github/
└── workflows/
    └── pr-checks.yml    # Single deliverable: PR validation workflow
```

**Structure Decision**: This feature produces a single YAML file. No source directories, no test files, no library code. The workflow file contains three jobs (detect, test, validate-pr-title) with inline bash scripts. This is the simplest possible structure for a CI workflow feature.

## Script Safety Design

*Addresses FR-010, FR-013, FR-014, FR-015 and Constitution Principle II.*

### Shell Configuration

The workflow MUST set `defaults.run.shell: bash` at the workflow level. Each inline `run:` block MUST begin with `set -euo pipefail`. GitHub Actions' default bash template (`bash --noprofile --norc -eo pipefail {0}`) provides `-e` and `-o pipefail` but omits `-u` (nounset). The explicit `set -euo pipefail` adds nounset protection and makes the safety contract self-documenting in the workflow file.

**Constitution Principle II reconciliation**: Principle II requires `#!/usr/bin/env bash` shebang and `chmod +x` for bash scripts. These requirements apply to standalone script files. Inline GitHub Actions `run:` blocks are not standalone files -- they are executed by the runner's shell invocation mechanism. The shebang and chmod requirements are therefore inapplicable to inline scripts. This is documented rather than treated as a principle violation.

### Script Injection Prevention (FR-013)

User-controlled GitHub Actions context expressions MUST be passed to inline scripts via intermediate environment variables, never by direct `${{ }}` interpolation in the script body. This follows the [GitHub-recommended mitigation for script injection](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/security/secure-use).

**Pattern for PR title validation:**

```yaml
- name: Validate PR title
  env:
    TITLE: ${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}
  run: |
    set -euo pipefail
    PATTERN='^(feat|fix|chore|docs|refactor|test)(\(.+\))?!?: .+$'
    if [[ "$TITLE" =~ $PATTERN ]]; then
      echo "PR title is valid: $TITLE"
    else
      echo "ERROR: PR title does not match..."
      exit 1
    fi
```

The `$TITLE` variable is double-quoted in the `[[ =~ ]]` test to prevent word splitting. The regex pattern is stored in a variable (`$PATTERN`) and used unquoted on the right side of `=~` to ensure bash interprets it as a regex (quoting the right side of `=~` forces literal string matching).

**Context expressions requiring env var treatment:**
- `${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}` -- user-controlled, MUST use env var
- `${{ github.base_ref }}` -- controlled by repository settings, low risk but using env var for consistency is recommended
- `${{ matrix.plugin }}` -- derived from git diff output filtered through plugin detection; safe because plugin directory names are constrained to kebab-case (`^[a-z][a-z0-9]*(-[a-z0-9]+)*$`) by Constitution Principle I. Direct interpolation in path expressions (e.g., `${{ matrix.plugin }}/tests/run-all.sh`) is acceptable.

### Git Diff Output Safety (FR-014)

The detect job parses `git diff --name-only` output to extract changed top-level directories. The safety concern is filenames with spaces, newlines, or special characters.

**Mitigations:**
1. **Top-level directory extraction**: The script extracts only the first path component via `cut -d'/' -f1`, which operates on each line. Since plugin directory names are kebab-case (Constitution Principle I), the extracted directory names themselves never contain spaces.
2. **Deduplication**: Uses `sort -u` on extracted directory names.
3. **Plugin filtering**: Uses `test -f "$dir/.claude-plugin/plugin.json"` with proper quoting around `$dir`.
4. **Scope of risk**: While file paths within plugin directories could theoretically contain spaces, the script only uses the top-level directory name (guaranteed safe by naming convention) for all downstream operations. The full file paths from `git diff` are never passed to commands that would break on spaces.

**Decision**: NUL-delimited output (`git diff -z`) is not required because the script only uses the first path component which is constrained to safe characters. Adding `-z` and `xargs -0` would add complexity without benefit (YAGNI, Constitution Principle VI).

### ReDoS Analysis (FR-015)

The PR title regex `^(feat|fix|chore|docs|refactor|test)(\(.+\))?!?: .+$` has been evaluated for ReDoS safety.

**Engine**: Bash `[[ =~ ]]` uses the system's POSIX Extended Regular Expression (ERE) library (glibc `regcomp`/`regexec` on Linux), which uses a backtracking NFA engine.

**Analysis**: The pattern is **ReDoS-safe** because:
1. **No nested quantifiers**: The pattern contains no constructs like `(a+)+` or `(a*)*` that create exponential backtracking paths.
2. **Bounded greedy match**: The `.+` inside `(\(.+\))` is bounded by literal `\(` and `\)` characters. On non-matching input, the outer `?` makes the entire group optional, and the engine simply skips it without backtracking into it.
3. **No overlapping alternatives**: The type alternation `(feat|fix|...)` has no common prefixes that could cause ambiguous matching (no two types start with the same character except `f` in `feat`/`fix`, which diverge at character 2).
4. **Single trailing quantifier**: The trailing `.+$` is a single unbounded quantifier anchored at end-of-line, which cannot interact with other quantifiers to create exponential paths.
5. **Input constraints**: PR titles are single-line (GitHub platform constraint, max ~256 characters), further limiting any linear backtracking cost.

**Scope note on `.+` in scope group**: The greedy `.+` inside `(\(.+\))` matches any characters including `)`, which means `feat(a)b): desc` would match with scope `a)b`. This is intentional -- the spec does not restrict scope content to specific characters, and overly restrictive scope matching would add complexity without value (YAGNI).

### Exit Code Semantics

| Job | Exit Code | Meaning |
|-----|-----------|---------|
| detect | 0 | Plugin detection completed (may output `[]` for no plugins) |
| detect | non-zero | Script error (e.g., `git diff` failed); `set -e` propagates |
| test | 0 | All tests passed for this plugin |
| test | 1 | Test failures detected by `tests/run-all.sh` |
| test | 2 | Test runner (`tests/run-all.sh`) not found (FR-012) |
| test | non-zero (other) | Unexpected script error; `set -e` propagates |
| validate-pr-title | 0 | PR title matches conventional commits pattern |
| validate-pr-title | 1 | PR title does not match (expected failure, actionable error shown) |
| validate-pr-title | non-zero (other) | Unexpected script error; `set -e` propagates |

**Job dependency propagation**: The test job uses `needs: [detect]`. If detect fails, GitHub Actions skips the test job by default. This is the desired behavior -- a broken detect job should not allow tests to silently pass.

## Error Message Design

*Addresses FR-007, FR-016, FR-017, FR-018, FR-019, FR-020 and SC-005.*

### Validate-PR-Title Error Output (FR-007, FR-016, FR-020)

When the PR title fails regex validation, the job MUST:
1. Echo the contributor's actual title so they can see what was rejected
2. Show the expected format pattern
3. List valid type prefixes
4. Provide at least one concrete example
5. Emit the error summary via `::error::` workflow command for Checks tab visibility

**Template:**

```bash
echo "::error::PR title does not match Conventional Commits format. See job log for details."
echo ""
echo "ERROR: PR title does not match Conventional Commits format."
echo ""
echo "  Your title: \"$TITLE\""
echo ""
echo "Expected format: type(scope): description"
echo "  - type must be one of: feat, fix, chore, docs, refactor, test"
echo "  - scope is optional: type: description is also valid"
echo "  - breaking changes: add ! before the colon: type(scope)!: description"
echo ""
echo "Examples:"
echo "  feat(speckit-pro): add new coaching command"
echo "  fix: resolve session timeout"
echo "  docs: update README"
echo "  feat!: breaking API change"
```

On success, the job logs: `PR title is valid: $TITLE`

### Test Job Error Output (FR-017, FR-018, FR-020)

**Job naming (FR-018):** The test job MUST set `name: test (${{ matrix.plugin }})` so each matrix entry appears with its plugin name in the Checks UI.

**Missing test runner (FR-017):**

```bash
echo "::error::Plugin '${{ matrix.plugin }}' has no test runner at ${{ matrix.plugin }}/tests/run-all.sh"
echo "ERROR: Plugin '${{ matrix.plugin }}' has no test runner."
echo "  Expected: ${{ matrix.plugin }}/tests/run-all.sh"
echo "  Every plugin directory must contain a tests/run-all.sh script."
exit 2
```

**Test failure:** When `bash tests/run-all.sh` exits non-zero, the exit code propagates via `set -e`. The `tests/run-all.sh` script itself produces detailed test output (assertions, pass/fail counts). No additional error formatting is needed at the workflow level because the test runner already produces actionable output.

### Detect Job Logging (FR-019)

The detect job MUST log its findings:

```bash
# After filtering to plugin directories:
if [ ${#plugins[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "No plugin directories changed in this PR. Test job will be skipped."
else
  echo "Changed plugin directories: ${plugins[*]}"
fi
```

This ensures the PR author can see why the test job was skipped or which plugins were selected for testing.

### GitHub Actions Annotations (FR-020)

The workflow uses `::error::` workflow commands ([GitHub Docs](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/workflows-and-actions/workflow-commands)) to surface failure summaries in the PR Checks tab. The annotation limit (10 errors per step, 50 per workflow) is well within budget for this workflow's failure modes.

The `::error::` command is used for:
- Invalid PR title (validate-pr-title job)
- Missing test runner (test job, FR-012/FR-017)

Test suite failures are NOT annotated with `::error::` because the test runner produces its own detailed output. The job failure status alone is sufficient to surface test failures in the Checks UI.
